2008-06-01.title: Baghdad Jews Have Become a Fearful Few
2008-06-01.abstract: Just over 50 years ago, Iraq’s Jews numbered more than 130,000, but only seven or eight still remain, groups say.

2008-06-01.title: Relief Helicopter Crashes in China
2008-06-01.abstract: A Chinese military helicopter involved in relief operations crashed in heavy fog near the town of Yingxiu with 14 people on board, according to China’s official news agency, Xinhua.

2008-06-01.title: European Bloc at Standstill Pending a Vote in Ireland
2008-06-01.abstract: Ireland is set to vote on June 12 in a referendum that will decide whether the European Union finally gets a full-time president and a single, more powerful, foreign policy chief.

2008-06-01.title: Chile and Peru Vie in Spat Over Spud
2008-06-01.abstract: Peru and Chile’s argument over the origins of the potato is the latest in a long string of arguments on issues big and small.

2008-06-01.title: Swiss to Decide on Secret Votes by Public on Citizenship Candidates
2008-06-01.abstract: The referendum has been put forward by a right-wing party that wants to change naturalization by public approval to a secret vote, the result of which could not be challenged.

2008-06-02.title: U.S. Deaths in Iraq Fell Sharply in May
2008-06-02.abstract: The 19 American casualties in May were the lowest monthly total since the 2003 invasion, the military said.

2008-06-02.title: U.S. Remakes Jails in Iraq, but Gains Are at Risk
2008-06-02.abstract: Gains from improved U.S.-run jails are threatened as thousands of detainees are to be turned over to the Iraqis.

2008-06-02.title: Taliban Leader Flaunts Power Inside Pakistan
2008-06-02.abstract: An appearance by the reclusive Baitullah Mehsud shows the wide latitude Pakistan has granted the militants.

2008-06-02.title: Violence Erupts in Macedonian Election
2008-06-02.abstract: The violence and allegations of fraud marred parliamentary elections, which had been seen as a test of Macedonia’s democratic credentials as it seeks to join the European Union.

2008-06-02.title: 3 Czech Friends, Cast as Heroes and as Murderers
2008-06-02.abstract: The Czech government’s decision to honor three survivors of a bloody, but successful, attempt to flee communist Czechoslovakia more than 50 years ago has led to a searing debate.

2008-06-03.title: Routing the Taliban
2008-06-03.abstract: A Marine unit moved through a town in southern Afghanistan, where the Taliban is retreating to the Pakistani border.

2008-06-03.title: Progress Has Been Made in Fight Against AIDS, but Not Enough, U.N. Report Says
2008-06-03.abstract: Nearly a million people began life-prolonging drug treatment in developing countries last year, but new infections far outstrip efforts to treat the sick.

2008-06-03.title: Myanmar Rulers Still Impeding Access
2008-06-03.abstract: Relief agencies said Monday that they were still having difficulty reaching hundreds of thousands of cyclone survivors who are in urgent need of assistance.

2008-06-03.title: China Lists Dos and Don’ts for Olympics-Bound Foreigners
2008-06-03.abstract: The Beijing Organizing Committee has pulled together some eclectic advice for foreigners wishing to attend Olympic events.

2008-06-03.title: Britain: Dry Subways’ Rough Departure
2008-06-03.abstract: London’s new policy of banning alcohol on the subways got off to a poor start when a party called to mark the last night of legal drinking descended into a chaotic revelry.

2008-06-04.title: Creating a Melody From Fear of Failure
2008-06-04.abstract: Playing a challenging musical work proved to be eye-opening for students at a national conservatory in Kazakhstan.

2008-06-04.title: In a Crackdown, Zimbabwe Curbs Aid Groups
2008-06-04.abstract: The government has clamped down on aid groups it claims are backing the opposition, relief agencies say.

2008-06-04.title: China Moves Quickly in Drug Mystery
2008-06-04.abstract: The deaths of six hospital patients who received a blood-based drug at a hospital in Jiangxi Province has prompted an unusually swift response from authorities.

2008-06-04.title: Gates Approves of 3-Year Tours for U.S. Troops in South Korea
2008-06-04.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said he supports extending the tours of thousands of troops stationed in South Korea to three years and allowing their families to live with them.

2008-06-04.title: Briton Admits to Plot to Set Off Explosives at Heathrow Airport
2008-06-04.abstract: The trial into an alleged plot to blow up as many as seven trans-Atlantic airliners on a single day in 2006 is expected to last eight months in London.

2008-06-05.title: Leaders Speak of Their Own Issues at a Conference Addressing Food Shortages
2008-06-05.abstract: Leaders from around the world spoke about economic issues in their own countries and political priorities at a U.N. conference on food shortages.

2008-06-05.title: U.S. Not Seeking Permanent Iraq Bases, Ambassador Says
2008-06-05.abstract: The Bush administration is not maneuvering to set up permanent military bases in Iraq, Ambassador Ryan C. Crocker said.

2008-06-05.title: Explosions and Shootings Disrupt Relative Calm in Iraq
2008-06-05.abstract: Insurgents preparing to attack an American military base in Baghdad accidentally killed themselves and more than a dozen civilians on Wednesday.

2008-06-05.title: Malaysia and India Increase Price of Subsidized Fuel
2008-06-05.abstract: India and Malaysia risked the wrath of voters by raising the price of subsidized fuel, measures that could further weaken the governments of both countries.

2008-06-05.title: Venezuela: Killing of Executive Might Have Been a Mistake
2008-06-05.abstract: The police in Caracas said that the killing of a newspaper executive on Monday might have been a case of mistaken identity.

2008-06-06.title: 16 Monks Arrested in Tibet Bombings
2008-06-06.abstract: Police in Tibet have accused 16 monks of involvement in bombings, a police spokesman in Tibet said.

2008-06-06.title: Justice Dept. Investigating Deportation to Syria
2008-06-06.abstract: The Justice Department is reviewing a 2002 decision by department officials to send a Canadian citizen to Syria, where he was tortured, American officials said Thursday.

2008-06-06.title: Germany: A Newspaper Provokes
2008-06-06.abstract: A leftist German newspaper provoked outrage and condemnation for printing what many readers called a racial slur about Senator Barack Obama.

2008-06-06.title: Bush Overstated Iraq Evidence, Senators Report
2008-06-06.abstract: The report accuses President Bush and other top officials of overstating the Iraqi threat in building the case for war.

2008-06-06.title: France Salutes the Ultimate Couturier
2008-06-06.abstract: France’s powerful and glamorous held funeral services on Thursday for Yves Saint Laurent, who was regarded by some as the finest fashion-designer of the last half of the 20th century.

2008-06-07.title: Georgia Is Warned by Russia Against Plans to Join NATO
2008-06-07.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev of Russia warned his Georgian counterpart on Friday that Georgia’s joining NATO would deepen conflicts between the former Soviet states.

2008-06-07.title: Pakistan Police Say They Foiled Attack
2008-06-07.abstract: Pakistani authorities said that they had foiled an attack when police intercepted three vehicles laden with a ton of explosives and arrested three alleged suicide bombers.

2008-06-07.title: Rains Add to Flood Threat in Quake Area
2008-06-07.abstract: As heavy rains descended on northern Sichuan Province for the first time in a week, the government evacuated thousands of people from towns heavily damaged by the earthquake.

2008-06-07.title: Postage Paid: $2.2 Million
2008-06-07.abstract: A rare strip of three Brazilian postage stamps from 1843 has been sold for $2,185,000 at an auction to an anonymous telephone bidder.

2008-06-07.title: Zimbabwe Blocks Opposition’s Rallies and Again Detains Its Leader
2008-06-07.abstract: The police briefly detained the opposition’s leader, Morgan Tsvangirai, for the second time this week and directed his party to cancel political rallies.

2008-06-08.title: Afghan Heroin Suspect Was U.S. Asset, He Says
2008-06-08.abstract: Haji Bashir Noorzai, a Taliban commander, says he was duped into coming to the U.S. to help authorities fight the Taliban and was arrested for drug trafficking instead.

2008-06-08.title: Survivors in China Sift Rubble for the Past
2008-06-08.abstract: Residents seek anything of value that will allow them to piece together some of what was snatched by the quake.

2008-06-08.title: Giving Young French Muslims a Close Look at the U.S.
2008-06-08.abstract: A longstanding program that seeks to introduce future leaders from around the world to the U.S. has become part of an American effort to reach out to Europe’s Muslims.

2008-06-08.title: Musharraf Rejects Calls to Resign
2008-06-08.abstract: President Pervez Musharraf, a stalwart ally of the United States, has been under mounting pressure since his supporters lost parliamentary elections in February.

2008-06-08.title: Albinos, Long Shunned, Face Threat in Tanzania
2008-06-08.abstract: Albinos have become victims of what Tanzanian officials say is a growing criminal trade in albino body parts.

2008-06-09.title: Experts See Gains Against Asian Terror Networks
2008-06-09.abstract: Policing, improved intelligence and eroded public support have weakened Southeast Asian terrorist networks.

2008-06-09.title: Money Talks at Russian Forum as Business Leaders See Past Hurdles to Investing
2008-06-09.abstract: Convening in St. Petersburg, big Western companies sent the message that they may be worried about the political environment in Russia, but that they are there to stay.

2008-06-09.title: Chávez Urges Colombian Rebels to End Their Struggle
2008-06-09.abstract: The Venezuelan president’s call to Colombia’s Marxist guerrillas was a surprising policy shift, just months after he called for the rebels to be recognized as a legitimate insurgent force.

2008-06-09.title: Lost Letter Raises Questions About Mbeki’s Role in Zimbabwe
2008-06-09.abstract: The office of the South African president denied it had received or that there even existed a letter of criticism sent by the Zimbabwean opposition leader, Morgan Tsvangirai.

2008-06-09.title: A Whiff of Naples Arrives in Hamburg
2008-06-09.abstract: Having run out of landfill space, Naples, Italy, has found a temporary solution to its trash problem in Germany.

2008-06-10.title: Applying Iraq’s Lessons in an Afghan Village
2008-06-10.abstract: Previous operations to clear Taliban militants from the village of Hazarjoft yielded only short-lived success.

2008-06-10.title: France: Chirac Creates Foundation
2008-06-10.abstract: The former president of France announced the creation of a nonprofit organization to promote sustainable development and dialogue among cultures.

2008-06-10.title: Bush, in German Visit, Will Discuss Iran With Merkel
2008-06-10.abstract: While some German experts are playing down the importance of this visit, others say there are issues that need to be discussed sooner rather than later.

2008-06-10.title: U.S. Troops Causing Instability, Iran’s Religious Leader Tells Iraqi Premier
2008-06-10.abstract: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei told Iraq’s visiting prime minister, “The most fundamental problem of Iraq is the presence of the foreign forces.”

2008-06-10.title: Where the Growth of Lush Fairways Has No Bounds
2008-06-10.abstract: In South Korea, where golf courses are expensive and overbooked, virtual-reality golf has become a fast-growing pastime.

2008-06-11.title: Philippine Television Crew Believed to Be Kidnapped
2008-06-11.abstract: A news team from the Philippines’ largest TV network was believed to have been abducted by members of the militant group Abu Sayyaf, officials said.

2008-06-11.title: Questions on U.S. Beef Remain
2008-06-11.abstract: About 50 countries, including Korea, Taiwan and Japan, closed their doors to American beef after the first confirmed case of mad cow disease was found in December 2003.

2008-06-11.title: Austria: Ex-Captive No Longer in Coma
2008-06-11.abstract: The illness of the 19-year-old Austrian woman helped expose the secret of a 73-year-old man accused of locking several family members in a cellar for up to 24 years.

2008-06-11.title: Strong Action Urged to Curb Warming
2008-06-11.abstract: The scientific academies of 13 countries on Tuesday urged the world to act more forcefully to limit the threat posed by human-driven global warming.

2008-06-11.title: Korean Leader Considers Ways to Rework Government
2008-06-11.abstract: Speaking to businessmen at his office, President Lee Myung-bak gave his first comment on the massive outdoor rally against his four-month-old leadership.

2008-06-12.title: Pirates Terrorize Nigeria’s Fishing Fleet
2008-06-12.abstract: The waters off the 530-mile Nigerian coastline have been called the most dangerous in the world after a precipitous rise in the number of attacks over the past year.

2008-06-12.title: Terror Bill Passes Narrowly in Britain
2008-06-12.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown won a knife-edge victory when Parliament supported his push to extend to 42 days the time terrorism suspects can be held without charges.

2008-06-12.title: Nepal: Deposed King Leaves Palace
2008-06-12.abstract: The world’s last Hindu king, Gyanendra of Nepal, turned in his scepter and bird-of-paradise plumed crown, and he announced that he would live as a commoner.

2008-06-12.title: Video Draws Attention to Growing Violence Against Minorities in Russia
2008-06-12.abstract: The video shoved the problem of violence against ethnic minorities into the foreground of national discourse, if only for a short time.

2008-06-12.title: Some Shark Populations Collapsing
2008-06-12.abstract: Numbers of five species of sharks in the Mediterranean have dropped sharply over the last two centuries, a study says.

2008-06-13.title: Disputed Blast in Gaza Kills 5
2008-06-13.abstract: A large explosion destroyed a residential building in Gaza, killing five Palestinians, including a baby and four men known as Hamas militants. Israel’s military denied any involvement.

2008-06-13.title: Pentagon Shows Video of Strikes Tied to Deaths of 11 Pakistani Soldiers
2008-06-13.abstract: The two-minute video clip taken by a remotely piloted surveillance aircraft shows only a portion of the events that have strained relations with Pakistan.

2008-06-13.title: Japan, Seeking Trim Waists, Measures Millions
2008-06-13.abstract: A country not known for obesity has undertaken an ambitious campaign to slim down its citizenry.

2008-06-13.title: Group Says Satellite Images Prove Rights Abuses in Ethiopia
2008-06-13.abstract: Human rights advocates on Thursday said that Ethiopia has been committing “crimes against humanity” in its crackdown on insurgents in the country’s isolated eastern Ogaden desert region.

2008-06-13.title: Chinese Parents Call Off Quake Memorial After Official Warning
2008-06-13.abstract: Parents who lost children in a school collapse during the May 12 earthquake scrapped plans for a mourning ceremony after local officials warned them not to go through with it.

2008-06-14.title: Slimming Japan
2008-06-14.abstract: Japan has undertaken one of the most ambitious campaigns ever by a nation to slim down its citizenry.

2008-06-14.title: China: More Antiterrorism Drills
2008-06-14.abstract: The Chinese government began a final round of antiterrorism drills at various Olympic sites this week.

2008-06-14.title: Northern Japan Quake Kills 2 and Injures Dozens
2008-06-14.abstract: A powerful earthquake with a magnitude of 7.0 shook northern Japan on Saturday morning, killing two people and injuring dozens, the Japanese government said.

2008-06-14.title: China Increases Lead as Biggest Carbon Dioxide Emitter
2008-06-14.abstract: China is rapidly extending its lead over the United States as the world’s largest emitter of carbon dioxide, the main heat-trapping gas produced by human activity.

2008-06-14.title: Afghanistan: More Aid Is Promised
2008-06-14.abstract: At a two-day session of NATO defense ministers in Brussels, Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates pleaded with allies to fulfill pledges for troops, and they promised modest additions.

2008-06-15.title: A Year Reshapes Hamas and Gaza
2008-06-15.abstract: Hamas has spread its authority across all aspects of life, and Gazans have not, as Israel and the United States hoped, risen up.

2008-06-15.title: Rival Turns Up the Heat on Musharraf
2008-06-15.abstract: A senior government leader escalated the pressure on Pakistan’s President Pervez Musharraf to resign, suggesting at a large rally that he should be hanged.

2008-06-15.title: Bush Shows Optimism on Iraq Deal
2008-06-15.abstract: President Bush expressed confidence on Saturday that the United States and Iraq would agree on a new security arrangement this year.

2008-06-15.title: Free and Flush, Russians Eager to Roam Abroad
2008-06-15.abstract: A people who under Communism were rarely allowed to travel abroad are now seeing the world. And relishing it.

2008-06-15.title: Old Farming Habits Leave Uzbekistan a Legacy of Salt
2008-06-15.abstract: Seventeen years after the end of the Soviet Union, cotton is still king in Uzbekistan, where the environmental destruction linked to the diversion of rivers for irrigation continues unabated.

2008-06-16.title: Karzai Threatens to Send Soldiers Into Pakistan
2008-06-16.abstract: President Hamid Karzai warned that Afghan troops would cross the border in response to attacks by militant groups.

2008-06-16.title: In Iraq’s South, a Mission Has Dual Aims
2008-06-16.abstract: In an operation with military and political objectives, the Iraqi Army continued to assemble troops in and around the southern city of Amara on Sunday.

2008-06-16.title: Officials Fear Bomb Design Went to Others
2008-06-16.abstract: Four years after Abdul Qadeer Khan’s atomic black market operation was declared over, inspectors are wondering who may have received the blueprints for a nuclear weapon found on his computer network.

2008-06-16.title: Valentine Vester, Jerusalem Hotelier, Is Dead at 96
2008-06-16.abstract: Mrs. Vester was a Yorkshire homemaker who went to Jordanian Jerusalem in 1963 to take over a little hotel, the American Colony, and turned it into one of the city’s finest.

2008-06-16.title: No Rushing Talks on Pact With U.S., Iraqis Say
2008-06-16.abstract: It will take many weeks and more likely months before the agreement is finalized, according to people close to the negotiations.

2008-06-17.title: Israel Seems to Make Progress in Talks
2008-06-17.abstract: Israel appeared to be making headway on a possible prisoner exchange with Hezbollah, a second round of indirect talks with Syrian representatives in Turkey and a possible truce with Hamas.

2008-06-17.title: Ex-Pentagon Lawyers Face Inquiry on Interrogation Role
2008-06-17.abstract: Senior lawyers played a more active role than previously known in developing techniques approved for use at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, say officials familiar with an investigation.

2008-06-17.title: In Khmer Rouge Trial, Victims Will Not Stand Idly By
2008-06-17.abstract: Hundreds of Cambodians have applied to be recognized officially as victims of the Khmer Rouge and to bring civil cases against five aging former leaders.

2008-06-17.title: Zimbabwe: Opposition Official Still Held Without Charge
2008-06-17.abstract: Four days after Tendai Biti, the chief strategist and secretary general of Zimbabwe’s main opposition party, was arrested, authorities had not formally charged him.

2008-06-17.title: Sudan: Security Council Rebuke
2008-06-17.abstract: The United Nations Security Council adopted a statement calling on the Sudanese government to cooperate in bringing to trial people charged with war crimes.

2008-06-18.title: Brown Says Europe Will Tighten Iran Sanctions
2008-06-18.abstract: After talks with President Bush, Prime Minister Gordon Brown said that Britain would freeze Iranian assets and increase its troop strength in Afghanistan.

2008-06-18.title: Israel Agrees to Truce with Hamas on Gaza
2008-06-18.abstract: The prime minister and defense minister of Israel have agreed to an Egyptian-brokered cease-fire with Hamas for the Gaza area starting Thursday, Israel Radio reported.

2008-06-18.title: Chinese Quake Toll, 69,172, Is Just a Guess
2008-06-18.abstract: There have been many shortcoming to the process of accounting for the dead and missing in the earthquake that hit Sichuan Province on May 12.

2008-06-18.title: Fact Mirrors Fiction in African Coup Trial
2008-06-18.abstract: The plot to overthrow the government of Equatorial Guinea in 2004 was so improbable that it sounded like something out of a tale from the tropics, too outlandish even for Graham Greene.

2008-06-18.title: Japan and China Near Agreement on Offshore Gas Fields
2008-06-18.abstract: The agreement would allow Japanese investment in Chinese companies that would drill in a contested part of the East China Sea, Japanese media said.

2008-06-19.title: Blast at Baghdad Market
2008-06-19.abstract: Explosives stowed in a minibus obliterated part of a bustling marketplace and set ablaze a crowded apartment building.

2008-06-19.title: Israel Offers Lebanon Talks on Peace, and Land
2008-06-19.abstract: After a visit by Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice, Israel said all issues would be negotiable, including the status of a contested piece of land on the Lebanon border.

2008-06-19.title: E.U. Passes Tough Migrant Measure
2008-06-19.abstract: Lawmakers voted to allow undocumented migrants to be held in detention centers for up to 18 months and banned from European Union territory for five years.

2008-06-19.title: No Longer the Capital, but a Global Destination
2008-06-19.abstract: Bonn, the little city on the Rhine, has succeeded in the unlikely goal of remaking itself as a place of the future.

2008-06-19.title: Taiwan’s Leader Outlines His Policy Toward China
2008-06-19.abstract: President Ma Ying-jeou of Taiwan called on Wednesday for a rapid expansion of economic relations between Taiwan and mainland China over the next year or two.

2008-06-20.title: U.S. Said to Compromise on Beef for South Korea
2008-06-20.abstract: An agreement in which the U.S. would not export to South Korea beef from cattle more than 30 months old was sought to defuse South Korean furor over beef exports.

2008-06-20.title: Canada: Latest Foot Is a Hoax
2008-06-20.abstract: What appeared to be the sixth human foot to wash up on British Columbia’s shore since last August is actually an animal’s paw and seaweed stuffed into a sock.

2008-06-20.title: Oil Field Operation Suspended After Attack by Nigerian Rebels
2008-06-20.abstract: It was the first time that militants had managed to hit Royal Dutch Shell’s deep offshore platform in the Niger Delta region.

2008-06-20.title: South Koreans Press Anti-Government Protests
2008-06-20.abstract: Critics of President Lee Myung-bak said they would continue their protests despite his repeated apologies for a U.S. beef import deal.

2008-06-20.title: China Presses Injured Athletes in Quest for Gold
2008-06-20.abstract: Pressured by the nation’s athletic system, China’s Olympic aspirants are pushing themselves to their limits.

2008-06-21.title: A Tale of Three Cities
2008-06-21.abstract: Moises Saman, a photographer with The New York Times, visited Basra, Mosul and the Sadr City district of Baghdad in May, visually recording the security and social situation in each of Iraq’s three largest cities. (See the slide show.)

2008-06-21.title: Bush May End Term With Iran Issue Unsettled
2008-06-21.abstract: Barring a move by Israel, the Bush administration would not be likely to pursue military strikes against Iranian nuclear targets, according to administration officials.

2008-06-21.title: Hunger Strike by Hundreds of Islamists in Morocco Jails
2008-06-21.abstract: Many of the hunger strikers are among the 1,400 people convicted of terrorism charges in the wake of the 2003 terrorist attacks in Casablanca.

2008-06-21.title: Britain Sends Information on Suspect to the U.S.
2008-06-21.abstract: Britain said it gave American military prosecutors classified material on a prisoner’s allegations that he was tortured in Morocco after secretly being taken there by the C.I.A.

2008-06-21.title: Calls for More Zimbabwe Sanctions
2008-06-21.abstract: The European strategy, outlined at a summit, is to show concern without punishing ordinary Zimbabweans.

2008-06-22.title: Henry Chadwick, Scholar of Early Christianity, Dies at 87
2008-06-22.abstract: Professor Chadwick was an Anglican priest, professor, editor, translator and author whose historical voyages into early Christianity won praise for depth, insight and evenhandedness.

2008-06-22.title: At Odds With Air Force, Army Adds Its Own Aviation Unit
2008-06-22.abstract: As the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan have frayed the relationship between the services, the Army has organized its own surveillance unit.

2008-06-22.title: Assassins in Zimbabwe Aim at the Grass Roots
2008-06-22.abstract: At least 85 people have been killed in a campaign against the opposition in a presidential runoff, civic groups said.

2008-06-22.title: Inside a 9/11 Mastermind’s Interrogation
2008-06-22.abstract: The story of an analyst who interrogated Khalid Shaikh Mohammed without harsh tactics offers the closest look to date at the C.I.A.’s interrogation program.

2008-06-22.title: South Korea and U.S. Reach Deal on Beef Imports
2008-06-22.abstract: Protests continued despite an accord to restrict beef exports to cattle less than 30 months old to allay fears over mad cow disease.

2008-06-23.title: Agreements Elusive at Oil Talks in Saudi Arabia
2008-06-23.abstract: Oil prices rose after the Saudis pledged only a slight increase in production during an energy summit.

2008-06-23.title: Weather Slows Rescue After Typhoon Sinks Philippine Ferry
2008-06-23.abstract: Hundreds of people, including as many as 45 children, were feared dead after their boat capsized and sank.

2008-06-23.title: Agreements Are Elusive at Oil Talks in Saudi Arabia
2008-06-23.abstract: The Saudis pledged to make only a slight increase in oil production, and the summit made plain how few options are available to push prices down from record levels.

2008-06-23.title: Doubts in South Korea Halt Deal to Restart U.S. Beef Imports
2008-06-23.abstract: The accord on beef import from the U.S. into South Korea will be put on hold until public concern over the deal subsides, a South Korean official said.

2008-06-23.title: 2 Killed in Lebanon Clashes
2008-06-23.abstract: Supporters and opponents of the pro-Western government clashed Sunday in northern Lebanon.

2008-06-24.title: Democrats in Senate Seek to Block Deals for Iraqi Oil
2008-06-24.abstract: Charles E. Schumer is leading a group of Democratic senators to block a set of contentious no-bid oil contracts that Iraq has decided to award to the Western oil giants.

2008-06-24.title: Government Study Criticizes Bush Administration’s Measures of Progress in Iraq
2008-06-24.abstract: A new report by the Government Accountability Office says that the American plan for a stable Iraq is falling out of touch with the realities on the ground.

2008-06-24.title: American Envoy Is Linked to Arms Deal Cover-Up
2008-06-24.abstract: The American ambassador to Albania endorsed a plan to remove evidence of illegal Chinese origins on ammunition being shipped to Afghanistan, a military attaché told investigators.

2008-06-24.title: Germany: Calls to Remove U.S. Arms
2008-06-24.abstract: Social Democrats, who share in Germany’s governing coalition, and opposition parties called on the U.S. to remove all nuclear weapons stored in military bases in the country.

2008-06-24.title: Rights Groups Assail U.S. for Withholding Aid to Haiti, Citing Political Motives
2008-06-24.abstract: Human rights groups are strongly criticizing the withholding $54 million in U.S. loans to provide clean drinking water to Haiti as leverage for political change.

2008-06-25.title: After Philippine Ferry Wreck, Many Bodies and Few Survivors
2008-06-25.abstract: Rescuers reached the capsized Princess of the Stars and found many corpses inside the vessel. Only 62 of the more than 800 passengers are confirmed to have survived.

2008-06-25.title: Rockets Hit Israel, Breaking Hamas Truce
2008-06-25.abstract: Three Qassam rockets fired from Gaza on Tuesday struck the Israeli border town of Sderot in the first serious breach of a five-day-old truce between Israel and Hamas.

2008-06-25.title: A Fresh Take on a 19th-Century Gingerbread Village
2008-06-25.abstract: Nikolai Zakotnov vowed to rescue Tomsk and help the city defend an architectural heritage that is as charming as it is unexpected.

2008-06-25.title: New Allegations Revive an Italian Mystery of the 1980s
2008-06-25.abstract: The case of Emanuela Orlandi, who vanished in 1983, resuscitated after the news media reported a witness claiming that she had been kidnapped on the orders of an American archbishop.

2008-06-25.title: Congo: Former Warlord’s Trial Halted
2008-06-25.abstract: Lawyers at the International Criminal Court in The Hague fought over the possible release of Thomas Lubanga, whose trial on war crimes charges has been halted.

2008-06-26.title: For Iraqi Christians, Money Bought Survival
2008-06-26.abstract: Protection money paid to insurgents became a secret complication of life for Iraq’s Christian population.

2008-06-26.title: Thai Leader Under Fire Before Confidence Vote
2008-06-26.abstract: The Thai prime minister, Samak Sundaravej, was weakened by criticism over his handling of a territorial dispute with Cambodia, but appeared set to prevail in the vote.

2008-06-26.title: Olmert in Deal to Avoid Early Vote
2008-06-26.abstract: The move allowed the embattled prime minister of Israel to avert an imminent political crisis for his governing coalition, but also paved the way for his possible ouster.

2008-06-26.title: Activists Warned on Olympic Protests
2008-06-26.abstract: Political activists in Shanghai say they have been warned against expressing their opinions, speaking with foreigners, or visiting Beijing until after the Games.

2008-06-26.title: Zimbabweans Make Plea for Help as Runoff Nears
2008-06-26.abstract: Newly homeless Zimbabweans massed outside the South African Embassy in Harare, Zimbabwe, in a bid for help.

2008-06-27.title: Police Official and Guard Are Killed in Mexico
2008-06-27.abstract: The killing of a high-ranking commander in the federal police and a bodyguard appeared to be the latest attack against those waging a campaign against drug traffickers.

2008-06-27.title: Truce Is Strained as Militants Launch Rockets and Israel Keeps Goods Out of Gaza
2008-06-27.abstract: A truce between Israel and Hamas frayed when militants launched two rockets against Israel and Israel prevented goods from entering Gaza for a second day.

2008-06-27.title: Complex Ties Lead Ally Not to Condemn Mugabe
2008-06-27.abstract: South Africa’s president, Thabo Mbeki, is convinced that he can resolve the Zimbabwe crisis through patient negotiations with Robert Mugabe.

2008-06-27.title: Philippines: Typhoon Toll May Exceed 1,300
2008-06-27.abstract: The death toll from Typhoon Fengshen could reach more than 1,300, including the passengers and the crew of a ferry that capsized Saturday in the storm, officials said.

2008-06-27.title: Mexico: 14 Men to Be Compensated for Vasectomies
2008-06-27.abstract: Officials of Guerrero State have agreed to pay $3,400 each to 14 indigenous men who were coerced into having vasectomies a decade ago. The men will also get water storage tanks and cement to build new homes, Luis Barrera Rios, the state health secretary, told The Associated Press. During a visit to the village of El Camalote in 1998, health workers demanded that men with more than four children be sterilized, the National Human Rights Commission said last year in a report. The men said state workers had promised them new homes and scholarships for their children if they underwent the procedure.

2008-06-28.title: Violence and the Vote
2008-06-28.abstract: Thousands of Zimbabweans have been beaten or displaced from their homes in election-related violence.

2008-06-28.title: A Fearful Day in Zimbabwe
2008-06-28.abstract: Portraits of Zimbabweans outside the capital. With President Robert Mugabe the sole candidate, many voted only out of fear. Ink-stained fingers are proof of voting.

2008-06-28.title: North Korea’s Intent in Razing Tower Is Unclear
2008-06-28.abstract: Shifting dynamics between civilian officials and military leaders may have led to steps toward denuclearization.

2008-06-28.title: Russian Shift: A European Charm Campaign
2008-06-28.abstract: At his first summit meeting with the European Union, the new Russian president, Dmitri A. Medvedev, tried a new tactic on the Europeans: being nice to them.

2008-06-28.title: Mexico Accepts Anti-Narcotics Aid From U.S.
2008-06-28.abstract: With a deadly drug war breaking out all around, beleaguered Mexican officials on Friday welcomed $400 million in anti-narcotics assistance from Washington.

2008-06-29.title: Zimbabwe Faces Wider Sanctions Under Bush Plan
2008-06-29.abstract: After Zimbabwe’s widely denounced presidential runoff, President Bush announced new steps aimed at what he called an “illegitimate government.”

2008-06-29.title: After Brutality, Mugabe Offers an Olive Branch
2008-06-29.abstract: Facing denunciations of Zimbabwe’s election, including a call from President Bush for new penalties, Robert Mugabe now says he’s ready to talk to the opposition.

2008-06-29.title: 15,000 Protesters Defy Government in Seoul
2008-06-29.abstract: Protesters clashed violently with the police in protests over South Korea’s decision to lift an import ban on beef from the U.S.

2008-06-29.title: Pakistan Shells Islamic Militants Near Peshawar
2008-06-29.abstract: The strike was the first time the new civilian government resorted to military action against militants.

2008-06-29.title: Athletes Disabled by Wars Lead Iraqi Team in World Games
2008-06-29.abstract: War and hardship have not destroyed all of Iraq’s dreams for international competition. The country, which has been in three wars in two decades, has a robust Paralympic team.

2008-06-30.title: Sam H.F.J. Manekshaw Dies at 94; Key to India’s Victory in 1971 War
2008-06-30.abstract: Mr. Manekshaw was India’s best-known soldier and the architect of the country’s victory in the 1971 war with Pakistan that gave birth to Bangladesh.

2008-06-30.title: Malaysia Opposition Leader Accused of Sodomizing Aide
2008-06-30.abstract: Similar accusations against Anwar Ibrahim, the leader of Malaysia’s political opposition, led to the leader’s dismissal as deputy prime minister and jailing a decade ago.

2008-06-30.title: Anglican Conservatives, Rebelling on Gays, Will Form New Power Bloc
2008-06-30.abstract: Anglican conservatives declared that they would defy the church’s historic lines of authority and create a new power bloc within the church.

2008-06-30.title: U.S. Advised Iraqi Ministry on Oil Deals
2008-06-30.abstract: The disclosure, on the eve of the contracts’ announcement, is the first confirmation of direct American involvement in deals to open Iraq’s oil to commercial development.

2008-06-30.title: Mugabe Is Sworn In to Sixth Term After Victory in One-Candidate Runoff
2008-06-30.abstract: President Robert Mugabe’s main rival dismissed the inauguration, which came just hours after officials declared Mr. Mugabe the winner of a runoff election.
